name: Lincoln Log Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place sheets of foil on countertop for cooling cookies.
2. In a large bowl, combine brown sugar, shortening, water, and vanilla. Beat until well blended. Add eggs and beat again.
3. Sift together flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt. Mix into the creamed mixture at low speed until just combined. Stir in chocolate chips.
4. Form dough into logs that are 2-inches (5 cm) long and 3/4-inch (2 cm) wide. Place logs about 2 inches (5 cm) apart on an ungreased baking sheet.
5. Bake one baking sheet at a time for 7 to 9 minutes, or until cookies are set. Do not overbake. Allow cooling for 2 minutes on the sheet before transferring to foil to cool completely.
6. For icing, stir icing sugar and water until smooth. Divide into two bowls. Add cocoa to one bowl. Drizzle white icing over logs. Let set. Drizzle chocolate icing alongside the white icing. Makes: About 2-1/2 dozen cookies
